prompt.content:
To improve pipeline quality, the recruiting team wants to clean up applications in Greenhouse where candidate experience does not align with the job they applied for.

Identify active applications for roles where the job name contains “Nurse” or “Technician”, but where the candidate’s most recent job title does not contain the corresponding keyword (“Nurse” for Nurse roles, “Technician” for Technician roles). Reject these applications with Reject Application tool marking “Experience mismatch” as rejection reason, and "Data clean-up" as rejection note.

Create two new jobs in Greenhouse: 'Nurse - Talent Pool' and 'Technician - Talent Pool'.

From the set of applications rejected as part of this data clean-up, identify candidates whose most recent job title does contain “Nurse” or “Technician,” and create new applications for them in the appropriate job - 'Nurse - Talent Pool' or 'Technician - Talent Pool' (for example, candidates with “Nurse” in their most recent job title should be applied to Nurse - Talent Pool)

Do not round intermediate values, round final values to nearest integer. Share in console
1. Final number of active applications across all jobs post all movements
2. Final number of applications rejected with rejection note “Data clean-up” post all movements

rubrics.name: States that the final number of active applications across all jobs, post all... (ver_45ef4eea96b04ab6a06b8fdc04749521)

messages.content: States that the final number of active applications across all jobs, post all movements, is 134.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
1. Final number of active applications across all jobs, post all movements, is 134.

2. Final number of applications rejected with rejection note “Data clean-up”, post all movements, is 33.
